title: Modi's June 2026 Europe Swing: France Elevates India-Slovakia Strategic Engagement
lede: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will undertake a three-nation tour from June 13–18, 2026, visiting France and Slovakia at the invitation of Presidents Emmanuel Macron and Robert Fico, reinforcing India's strategic partnerships in Europe.

sections.context: Prime Minister Modi's June 2026 European itinerary comprises two distinct bilateral engagements. The French leg—scheduled for Nice on June 14 and Paris/Evian on June 16–18—follows the recent elevation of India-France relations to the level of a [[india-fr-india-and-france-elevate-ties-to-special-global-strateg-2026|Special Global Strategic Partnership earlier this year]]. During the Nice bilateral meeting with President Macron, both leaders will review the full spectrum of India-France ties and jointly inaugurate the 'Bharat Innovates' event, a signature initiative bringing together innovation startups and venture capital funds from India, France, and other countries. This event is timed to coincide with the India-France Year of Innovation, cementing the technology and innovation partnership between the two democracies. The second leg of Modi's tour will see him pay a State Visit to Slovakia from June 14–16, marking what the source indicates will be a significant diplomatic moment for India-Slovakia bilateral engagement.
sections.threads: This tour reflects India's deepening engagement with European strategic actors beyond traditional multilateral forums. The emphasis on innovation and technology partnerships with France signals alignment with New Delhi's broader Digital India and Atmanirbhar Bharat initiatives, positioning France as a key technology collaborator. The inclusion of Slovakia in Modi's European itinerary, announced as a State Visit, indicates India's intent to expand its Central European diplomatic footprint. These visits follow established patterns of [[india-fr-modis-three-nation-tour-france-elevates-india-to-specia-2026|Modi's multi-country European tours]] that have historically combined bilateral deepening with multilateral visibility. The timing also reflects India's strategy to engage with European Union member states individually while leveraging the France partnership as a gateway to broader EU engagement.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor three key developments over the next 6–18 months. First, the outcomes of the 'Bharat Innovates' event—particularly whether concrete venture capital flows or technology transfer agreements materialize between Indian startups and French/European funds. Second, the specific deliverables from the India-Slovakia State Visit, which could indicate whether India is establishing Slovakia as a Central European hub or engagement point. Third, whether this tour catalyzes broader EU-India strategic dialogue, particularly given France's current EU presidency rotation and its role in shaping European geopolitical strategy. The India-France Special Global Strategic Partnership elevation [[india-fr-france-and-india-elevate-to-special-global-strategic-pa-2026|established earlier in 2026]] will be tested by the substance of outcomes from these bilateral meetings, particularly in defence, technology, and multilateral coordination frameworks.
